title: Boat Rental in Lake Como: A Budget Guide for 2026
html:
Lake Como offers some of Europe's finest boating, but the perception of high costs often deters budget travellers. Renting a boat from Como city centre can start from as little as 170 EUR for a three-hour tour of the central basin, and platforms like BoatHire24 make it easy to compare prices and find affordable options.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Lake Como?

A self-drive motorboat for half a day costs between 200 and 400 EUR. Prices vary by season, boat size and location. Shared tours start around 60 EUR per person and typically last two hours. For a full-day private charter of a small speedboat, budget 500 to 800 EUR in low season, rising to 1,200 EUR in August.

  • Shared tour (2 hours): from 60 EUR per person
  • Small motorboat (half day, 4, 6 persons): 250, 400 EUR
  • Catamaran full day (up to 12 guests): 1,500, 2,500 EUR
  • Luxury crewed yacht (full day, 8 guests): 3,000, 5,000 EUR

To see real-time availability and prices, browse day charters in Lake Como on BoatHire24, where owners list their boats directly.

Where to set off in Lake Como

Most budget rentals depart from public marinas in the main towns. The lake has three principal basins; the central and southern areas are cheapest. Consider these launch points:

  • Como, the main transport hub, with several rental kiosks along the lakefront promenade.
  • Lecco, the south-eastern arm offers quieter waters and lower prices.
  • Menaggio, central location, ideal for exploring Bellagio and Varenna.
  • Dongo, a peaceful northern town with a municipally run dock and good wind shelter.
  • Gravedona, close to the Pian di Spagna nature reserve, popular for sailing.

Which boat to rent

Your choice depends on group size, licence requirements and budget. Here are the most common rental types:

  1. Self-drive speedboat, best for couples or small groups seeking freedom; up to 5 guests; from 250 EUR for three hours.
  2. Day cruiser (6, 8 m), ideal for families, with a small cabin and bimini; up to 8 guests; from 350 EUR for half day.
  3. Sailing yacht (30, 40 ft), quiet and eco-friendly; up to 6 guests; from 400 EUR for half day without skipper.
  4. Motor yacht (e.g. Princess V58), luxury with full crew; up to 10 guests; from 2,500 EUR for a full day. For details visit the Princess V58 charter page.
  5. Small rib / tender, the cheapest option for two, easy to handle; 2, 3 guests; from 150 EUR for two hours.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Lake Como?

No licence is required for boats under 40 hp in Italian inland waters. Many small speedboats and rips fall into this category. For larger vessels, an International Certificate of Competence (ICC) is standard. The rental operator will confirm exact requirements at booking. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Lake Como or list your own.

In high season (July and August) the lake fills with boats, so book at least two weeks ahead. The afternoon breva wind from the south can make the central basin choppy, so morning departures are usually calmer.

Seasonal tips for Lake Como

The best months for cheap rentals are May, June and September. High season runs from mid-July through August, when prices spike 30, 50%. The lake's afternoon wind, the breva, picks up around 2 pm and can reach 15 knots. Boats with smaller engines struggle against it, so plan your route accordingly.

At a glance

  • Price band: 60, 5,000 EUR
  • Peak months: July, August
  • Typical trip length: 2, 8 hours
  • Maximum guests: 2, 12
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Lake Como

Can I rent a boat for just an hour?

Most operators offer a minimum of two hours. A one-hour rental is rare unless you book a guided tour from a lakeside hotel.

What happens if the weather is bad?

Reputable companies will cancel or reschedule free of charge if a storm warning is issued. Check the policy before paying.

Is it cheaper to rent from Como or Bellagio?

Como tends to be cheaper due to more competition and easier public transport access. Bellagio locations charge a premium for convenience.

Do I need to pay a deposit?

Most rentals require a refundable security deposit of 300, 500 EUR, held on your credit card until the boat is returned undamaged.

Whether you are a first-time renter or an experienced boater, planning ahead keeps costs down in 2026. Browse the BoatHire24 marketplace to compare boats, read reviews, and even become a host if you own a vessel. Lake Como's shimmering waters await you.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Lake Como and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
